justification: Confidence calibration is present and systematically applied (high/low areas, assumptions listed) — meets that criterion well. Report content is correctly flagged as undefined with no invented metrics. Pippen's async recommendation is prominently featured. However, Shaq is effectively fully blocked: the first escalation says 'Resolve with MJ and Pippen before handing off to Shaq' and the second says Bird must provide specifics before 'any implementation.' The rubric requires the escalation to be partial, allowing Shaq to begin API scaffolding immediately — this output does not provide that actionable starting point. Additionally, the rubric expects API endpoint, format parameter, and date range as high-confidence items, but the output moved endpoint design and format to low-confidence due to the sync/async contradiction, meaning the high-confidence section doesn't match expected content.

observations.text: Excellent confidence calibration with structured high/low areas and explicit assumptions — goes beyond minimum requirements
Correctly refuses to invent report content or metrics, explicitly noting Bird's gap
Pippen's async concern elevated to a first-class architectural contradiction rather than buried as a note — arguably stronger than required
Spike option mentioned as one possibility for unblocking, showing awareness of partial-block pattern
Shaq is effectively fully blocked — 'Resolve with MJ and Pippen before handing off to Shaq' and 'any implementation is speculative' prevent immediate scaffolding work
Date range parameters, the one clearly agreed item, is in high-confidence, but API endpoint and format — which the rubric considers high-confidence — are moved to low-confidence/contested
No explicit 'what Shaq CAN implement now' vs 'what Shaq CANNOT implement' separation — the rubric specifically requires this distinction to enable partial progress
